marketState: neutral, cautiously bullish tilt from peak-season weather demand
summary: Front price ($2.911) sits almost exactly on modeled fair value ($2.923, FAIR status), so there is no near-term mispricing edge to trade today. The strongest signal in the data is weather: 8-16 day cooling demand is running +21.8% above the 5-year norm and sits near the top of its seasonal range (rangePos 0.97), and the fair-value curve itself drifts up from $2.923 now to $3.042 by +21 days, implying the model expects the heat-driven demand pull to firm prices into August. That bullish read is offset by a +180 Bcf storage surplus vs the 5-year average and a bearish +22 Bcf build surprise this week, plus seasonality that shows only a 38% chance of higher prices at the 3-4 week mark with low reliability (59pp spread). Managed money is net short at just the 8th percentile of its 5-year range, a stretched positioning that could fuel a short-covering rally but could equally reflect funds correctly leaning into the supply overhang. The futures curve is in mild backwardation front-to-next (-$0.035) consistent with cooling-season roll-off, but flips into a large winter contango (Dec $3.735, Jan $4.16), confirming the market already prices a seasonal ramp. Technicals are split by timeframe - 15m/1h lean neutral-to-bullish while 4h/1d/1w show bearish trend with a 50<200 EMA cross.

longTerm.stop: $2.65 (below lower-quartile of fair-value band; invalidates the weather-demand thesis)
longTerm.tp2: $3.45 (upper region of fair-value band, if peak-summer heat and winter contango pull continue)
longTerm.riskReward: ~1:0.8 to TP1, ~1:2.2 to TP2 (risk ~$0.25 from a $2.90 entry)
longTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk; size so a $0.25/MMBtu adverse move equals 1R
longTerm.rationale: 8-16D weather demand is +21.8% vs 5y and near the top of its range, the fair-value curve rises to $3.042 by +21 days, and the futures curve already prices a steep winter contango (Dec $3.735, Jan $4.16), all arguing for higher prices into peak cooling season and beyond; extreme net-short COT positioning (8th percentile) adds short-covering upside risk. This is tempered by a +180 Bcf storage surplus, a bearish weekly storage surprise, and low-reliability seasonality showing only a 38% chance of higher prices at 3-4 weeks, so conviction is capped at medium rather than high.
longTerm.invalidation: A drop through $2.65, a fade in the 8-16D weather demand anomaly back toward normal, or another larger-than-forecast storage build would invalidate the long thesis.

shortTerm.rationale: Front price is essentially at fair value (spread -$0.012, status FAIR) so there is no near-term mispricing to fade, and technicals conflict sharply by timeframe: 15m/1h neutral-to-bullish vs 4h/1d/1w bearish trend with a 50<200 EMA cross. The front-next curve is only mildly backwardated (-$0.035), too small to be an actionable term-structure signal on its own.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either a pullback toward the $2.70-2.75 lower fair-value zone to buy in line with hot 1-7D weather (+13% vs 5y), or a confirmed breakdown below $2.75 with 1h/4h/1d trend alignment to short.

keyDrivers: 8-16D weather demand +21.8% vs 5y, rangePos 0.97 (near seasonal top) - strong cooling-demand pull
Front price at fair value now ($2.911 vs $2.923 fair) but fair-value curve rising to $3.042 by +21D
Managed money net short at 8th percentile of 5y range - stretched positioning, short-covering risk
Storage surplus +180 Bcf vs 5y with a bearish +22 Bcf build surprise this week caps upside
Futures curve backwardated front-to-next but strongly contangoed into winter (Dec/Jan), confirming seasonal repricing already underway
risks: Seasonality reliability is Low (59pp 10-90% spread) - the seasonal edge itself is weak this week
Higher-timeframe technicals (4h/1d/1w) show a bearish trend and death cross, risking further downside before the fundamentals thesis plays out
Extreme COT short positioning may reflect valid bearish fundamentals (storage surplus) rather than a pure contrarian setup
